description: Schema describing the recommended heading structure of the body of knowledge-base topic markdown files. The 'body' is everything below the YAML frontmatter; the frontmatter contract itself lives in ./frontmatter.config.json. Topics MUST follow the heading hierarchy declared under `tiers.X.sections`, in the order listed.

conventions.topLevelHeading: Do not include a level-1 heading; the frontmatter `title` is the implicit H1.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.description: First section of the component topic. 1-3 sentences summarising what the component is and the user-facing problem it solves. Do not restate the frontmatter `title` (the component name) verbatim and do not introduce a level-1 heading ('# ...') anywhere in the body — the frontmatter `title` is the implicit H1.

sections.heading: When to use
sections.description: User-facing scenarios in which this component is the right choice. Describe the interaction patterns and UI contexts the component is designed for.

sections.heading: When to not use
sections.description: Scenarios where this component is the wrong choice. Call out adjacent components in the design system that should be used instead, and link to them.

sections.heading: Guidelines
sections.description: How to use the component correctly. Organised into the level-3 sub-sections listed below; cover at minimum developer integration and the public API surface.

sections.heading: Developer usage
sections.description: How developers integrate the component: package import path, the custom-element tag, framework wrappers (React etc.), required slotted children, and a minimal working markup example.

sections.heading: Content guidance
sections.description: Guidance for the content rendered inside or alongside the component — slotted text, labels, icons, helper text — covering tone, length, casing, and editorial conventions.

sections.heading: Property/Attribute details
sections.description: Detailed reference for the component's public properties and attributes that need extra explanation beyond the generated API table: accepted values, defaults, interaction with other properties, and intended use. Do not duplicate the auto-generated API table — add context the table cannot express.

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.description: Known limitations of the component, edge cases (RTL, theming, nested usage), intentional non-goals, and behaviours that may surprise consumers.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.description: Additional supporting context, history, or rationale about the component that does not fit the other sub-sections.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.description: Accessibility contract for the component. Split into what the component handles internally and what the consumer must supply when adopting it.

sections.heading: Built-in features
sections.description: Accessibility behaviours the component implements out of the box: keyboard interaction, focus management, screen-reader semantics, and any reduced-motion or high-contrast handling. Consumers can rely on these without additional work.

sections.heading: Internal ARIA managed by the component
sections.description: ARIA roles, states, and properties that the component sets, updates, or removes on its internal DOM (for example `role`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`). Consumers must not override these unless explicitly documented as overridable.

sections.heading: Implementation requirements
sections.description: Accessibility responsibilities the consumer must satisfy when adopting the component for the resulting UI to be accessible.

sections.heading: General
sections.description: General accessibility requirements that don't fit under the other sub-sections, such as ensuring passing aria related attributes to the component, etc.

sections.heading: Labelling
sections.description: How the consumer must label the component for assistive technologies: which attribute or slot to use (visible label, `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, slotted label element), when each is appropriate, and examples for common cases such as icon-only usage.

conventions.topLevelHeading: Do not include a level-1 heading; the frontmatter `title` (the component name) is the implicit H1.
conventions.sectionOrder: Top-level sections must appear in this order: Overview, Guidelines, Accessibility. Sub-sections must appear in the order listed under their parent. Unknown headings are discouraged; add new sections to this config first.
